reviews.review_text: It was a great spot to stop about half way on our trip to suncity tenpin bowling alley place was nice and quiet and very clean

We stayed in the park overnight. Camp's location was great, the cabin was clean and very quiet as the other two cabins on each sides were empty (guests allocation was right). Luna from reception called in advance to inform about the check in process, we settled smothly without any issues. I wish they had kids play equipment handy, but the rest was fine.
Park is a bit run down..only had overnight stay..reception staff were great and very helpful ...waterfront was beautiful and the facilities were fantastic to sit and watch the barges and boats
Great boat ramp, friendly locals
Run down,  unhospitable like others we met, only place left around Port Maquarie peak season Christmas time that had camp sites left we now know why!
nice and quiet fishing was great on there private wharf a little outdated could use a spruce up
Just visiting
Bike was stolen

Awesome Camping Ground! They took us in after 5pm even though the office is normally already closed at this time. Really helped us out of an unpleasant situation when our car had just broken down. Staff is friendly, helpful and forthcoming. They gave us a powered site to a fair price. The amenities are tidy and get cleaned every morning. The camp kitchen is very comfortable and offers plenty of space to bbq, a fridge and a stove. They even offered help with our car, recommending mechanics. Very good experience overall.

Very friendly and accommodating.
4 days working on a festival.
No problem with the early starts and late finishes
Great chill-out place to stay, by the river, close to town facilities. Pet free, so quiet, and no poop laying about.

Absolutely disgusting, cobwebs 🕸️ in every corner, leaking taps, holes in curtains, tv didn’t work. Cabin In disrepair. Never again. Great location , nice riverside ambience   But overall condition of cabin was terrible sadly, mildew outside on walls,

Older park but serviceable, right on river next to car ferry
Bikepacker note the ferry is free for bikes to access national Parks and hathead ect

Have just spent 3 nights here in an ensuite cabin. yes it is an older cabin with lots of timber veneer walls etc but it was clean and everything worked as it should. The bed and pillows were comfy and clean. We will definitely stay again, thankyou Edgewater for an affordable, quiet and peaceful stay.
Absolutely stunning spot to stay and I have to say the cleaner was the best. She went above and beyond to make our stay comfortable and is so lovely ❤️ we will be coming back for sure 🌸🌸🌸

Great holiday destination, family friendly environment. We stayed a week over Christmas on a powered site, amenities are a bit outdated but clean and serviced regularly. Can't fault the location of this place, even though it's not Town Centre, it's less than a 5 minute drive and walking distance to Aldi, food spots & other department stores. Our toddler loved watching the planes fly over and even though they were regular they weren't noisy! We loved the pool and staff were friendly, accommodating & shared great kid friendly activities nearby to go to. Keeping this place in mind for the future.

We (2 adults) camped 2 nights in our caravan. I didn't like being situated under a huge widow maker gum tree, since it was very windy and i was scared of branches dropping. We had already set-up when i noticed the trees and the lady at reception would have left so i didnt ask to move. We sat outside well away from the drop zone and survived. This huge branch did land very close to our van. If i were to return i would ask for one of the grassy sites with no trees. The restrooms are tired and need renovation to make the shower stalls more generously sized. The pool looks nice but it's a pity that its next to the busy road. The standout feature is the fantastic new public shelter and covered deck directly on the river. We enjoyed drinks on the deck. See photo from deck.

We were only staying for one night as in Port Macquarie for the weekend.
The cabin we stayed in was clean and tidy but basic and dated. The bunks beds were not made(no sheets on them but a bed cover on it). The holiday park on a whole seems a bit dated and tired looking.
Always liked this park. Central to everything
We found the park very good. Good service, nice location and well maintained.

This park is in a great location with a small walk to a riverside platform  with protection and seats. Lovely sunset from here. Sites are large with amenities nearby. Amenties and facilities are sjowing their age but overall very good. We were there in late July and found it very quite.
Nice little park bit out of town its right on thr river.The amenities are alittle outdated but do the job we stay here regulary.

I'm sorry to say the day we got there our first  cabin we was in it had ants 🐜 crawling under the bunk beds so we had to be moved to another cabin  and when we were in that cabin we had the tv goes on us  the office lady's could not crack a smile  so that will be last time I will be bringing my family back and to be honest I wouldn't bring my dog here

We arrived just before closing time because it was raining. There was an elderly lady attending the reception, who, upon seeing us arrive, exited through another door and got into a car to leave. When we stopped her and politely asked if we could stay since we had nowhere else to go, she said it wasn't possible and left. Terrible service for a place that should primarily be of help to people in need of a place to stay, especially on a rainy day. Awful, don't go there.

RUDE STAFF, i can tell from other reviewes that this is common that teh manegemtn is rude and def wont recommend for you to stay here. Out dated rooms, all fly screens are broken. The cabins do not offer clean beds or linen. Avoid supporting these guys by paying them for their cheap accomodation
Nice place good location. There is an airport nearby with little planes coming and going but otherwise a nice little spot

Very nice, well maintained holiday park. Located well, out of the rat race. But still close enough to everything you need on a holiday or short break. Amenities are well looked after. Nice quiet location.
Recommend for a couple or families.
We are enjoying our stay in the peaceful van area. We are here in sad circumstances and have been shown nothing but kindness and empathy.

I tried booking in to stay, lady at the front counter was rude and basically telling me she didn't want my business and they don't do extended stay(even for work)
I walked out regretted even walking in.....

The only good thing about the place is the location and views of the water. The female owner is rude and doesn't give clear instructions getting snappy when she had to come out because she didn't explain our keys were in an unlocked free for anyone to take cabin key.
If the service wasn't bad enough the state of the 4 cabins were horrendous! 1 room had poop dried on the wall, pubic hair in the drains and dirty linen that wasn't on the beds on arrival just slapped on the end. Another cabin had dirty oiled linen and all the rooms were dirty and dusty. The only heating was a fan heater that you could not keep going during the night for fear of a fire starting and that was if it wasn't throwing sparks or rusty pins on the plugs 😳
100% do not recommend nor will I ever stay here again. It would have been cheaper to stay elsewhere especially when it's $10/set of linen needed and it comes dirty covered in hair and lube/oil stains.

Thought it would be nice to get out of the hustle and bustle of Port Macquarie in the long weekend. The park is certainly quiet and still not far from the town centre.|However, it is in need of some TLC. Great location right on the river and the camp kitchen overlooking the river is excellent.|Many parts of the park where the residents live are unkept and looked like a Zombie Apocalypse movie. Laundries have old machines,over half not working. Suggest using a local laundrette. Amenities have seen better days and signs advising of gutter damage gave little confidence. Swimming pool water was clean but the bottom was grimy and looked like it needed a good  clean.|It was also the most expensive park we used on a five week trip to Queensland and back and was no where near like value for money.|Needs a really good revamp to bring up to standard, or in the interim a reduction in prices being charged.

Our large extended family gathered at Edgewater and we enjoyed a long weekend together around the grassy area and anchorage room. The BBQ area was great for catering and the Sandy beach was a lovely meeting spot for the small children to play. The larger cabins were spacious and clean and had everything we needed. Beautiful views of the river too.
